title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT)
An Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) is a debit or credit transaction. Debit Transactions are known as Pre-authorized Debits (PADs). Credit Transactions are known as Direct Deposits.
Business Day
A business day is a weekday including Monday to Friday except for statutory holidays that fall on or are observed on a weekday.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
CPA
CPA means the Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
Credit Transaction
A credit transaction is a credit Payment Item exchanged in an AFT File as defined in CPA Standard 005. Also known as a Direct Deposit.
Debit Transaction
A debit transaction is a Pre-authorized Debit within the meaning of Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits, exchanged in an AFT file as defined in CPA Standard 005.
Direct Clearer
A Direct Clearer is a member of the CPA that meets the requirements of the association and settles for clearing items through a settlement account at the Bank of Canada.
Direct Deposit
A Direct Deposit is an AFT credit transaction such as a payroll deposit.
Due Date
The Due Date is the date on which the Payor is to be debited or the date on which the Payee is to receive payment.
Error Correction
An Error Correction is an AFT transaction used to correct an error made by the Originator or the Originating Direct Clearer. An Error Correction corrects a transaction(s) posted to the recipientâs account(s) in error. Error Corrections apply to both transactions and AFT files.
Financial Institutions File (FIF)
The Financial Institutions File (FIF) is an electronic directory of branches of deposit-taking institutions in Canada and is maintained by Payments Canada. It includes information to facilitate the processing and exchange of payment items between deposit-taking institutions in Canada.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
File Creation Number
A File Creation Number is a consecutive number automatically assigned to an AFT file during preparation for transmission. Central 1 uses the File Creation Number for verification purposes because files must be received in sequential order.
File Error Correction
A File Error Correction is an AFT transaction that is sent to the Processing Direct Clearers to correct transactions that were posted to the recipientsâ accounts in error. A File Error Correction transaction is generated for each transaction in the original AFT file. A File Error Correction is sometimes referred to as a Mass Error Correction or a File Reversal.
File Recall
A File Recall is when an Originator requests the return of an AFT file that has not yet been processed at Central 1. See Recall.
Lead Time
Lead Time is a reference to the number of days before the Due Date that Central 1 must receive an AFT file. The Lead Time is generally two business days, but it may be shorter in some cases based on Serviceability Code â0â.
Notice of Change (NOC)
A Notice of Change (NOC) is a non-financial transaction created by the Receiving Financial Institution. It informs the payment Originator of changes to the payment routing information.
ORS
The Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of AFT transactions and the creation of AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Originating Direct Clearer
The Originating Direct Clearer delivers AFT files to another Direct Clearer. Central 1 is the Originating Direct Clearer for financial institutions in BC and Ontario.
Originator
An Originator is the financial institution or business member that creates an AFT file.
Originator ID
An Originator ID is the 10-digit number assigned by Central 1 that identifies the Originator of the AFT file.
Payee
The Payee is the entity for which the credit transaction is intended.
PaymentStream AFT
PaymentStream
AFT allows users to create AFT Transactions and upload AFT files.
Payor
The Payor is the entity for which the debit transaction is intended.
Pre-authorized Debit (PAD)
A Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) is an AFT transaction issued by a Payee or member Payee that is drawn on an account of a Payor (e.g., mortgage payment, strata payment). See Debit Transaction.
Pre-notification
Pre-notification is the required Written Notice from the Payee or member Payee to the Payor, indicating the amount and date of a Pre-authorized Debit (PAD). Pre-notification is required prior to the date of the actual PAD, and it is also required to make a change to the amount or date of a PAD.
Processing Direct Clearer
The Processing Direct Clearer is the Clearer that processes AFT transactions received from an Originating Direct Clearer.
Recall
A Recall is a request to return an AFT transaction that has not been posted to the receiverâs account. A Recall applies to individual credit transactions and to AFT files. Recalled AFT files can contain both credits and debits. Individual debits can only be recalled if Central 1 has not sent the transaction to the data centre of the Receiving Financial Institution. See File Recall.
Receiving Financial Institution
The Receiving Financial Institution is the financial institution that receives AFT transactions from another financial institution.
Reject
A Reject is a transaction in the AFT file that cannot be processed by Central 1, the other data centre, or the Receiving Financial Institution.
Run
A Run is the Central 1 processing period. In BC, Canadian AFT transactions are processed up to four Runs each calendar day and USD AFT transactions are processed in one Run each business day. In Ontario, Canadian AFT transactions are processed up to 3 Runs each calendar day and USD AFT transactions are processed in one Run each business day.
Serviceability Code
The Serviceability Code is a code on the Financial Institutions File (FIF) that represents the number of business days required to credit a Payeeâs account with an AFT credit transaction on the date those funds are to be made available.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Settlement Date
The Settlement Date is the date on which Central 1 posts a transaction to the financial institution. This is usually the Due Date.
Transit Number
The Transit Number consists of the Branch Number (5 digits) plus the Institution Number (3 digits). Each financial institution has a unique Transit Number (e.g., 80912010).
Transaction Recall
See Recall.
Written Notice
Any form of representing or reproducing words in visible form, including an electronic document, provided the electronic document is under the control of the intended recipient, the information contained in the electronic document is substantially in the same form as a paper copy, and the information contained in the document is accessible if requested.
END
